excerpt: The Imāms of the Pure Household taught that one must first perform wuḍūʾ (ablution), then remove any external impurity, and thereafter pour water upon the entire body...
source_location_hint: section on ritual purity and ghusl

excerpt: The Commander of the Faithful has transmitted in his Four Hundred Maxims of Conduct that no believer should surrender to sleep in a state of major impurity...
The ghusl of Friday holds primacy among the twelve Sunnaic baths by virtue of its nobility and rank...it occupies a unique station between obligation and recommendation...
source_location_hint: commentary on Friday ghusl and cosmic correspondence

excerpt: Major ritual impurity occurs when a man's procreative fluid enters his wife; infertility results when the fluid is emitted but does not enter...One should therefore hasten to purify oneself when obligatory...
In another reading, ghusl signifies the removal of all doubts and suspicions through the knowledge of the Imām, for doubts arise only from false learning...
source_location_hint: esoteric commentary on major impurity and knowledge

excerpt: The example of iḥtilām is one who unintentionally and by error speaks something of taʾwīl, then is startled...Such forced perversion leads to external and internal disease.
source_location_hint: discourse on interpretation and spiritual practice

excerpt: The bodies of the Imāms are constituted of pure life; all they consume throughout their days becomes life itself. The believer receives the sustenance of the Imāms at three moments...
source_location_hint: chapter on spiritual sustenance and the Imāmate

excerpt: God Most High brings forth to us the fruit of that virtue as water, and we bathe ourselves in it...God possesses the reckoning of every atom—and thus it is said that God is swift in reckoning.
source_location_hint: on divine justice and the preservation of deeds

excerpt: When a man's procreative seed enters a woman's womb and unites with her seed, they become one...These secretions continuously reach the developing seed, nourishing it to grow...
source_location_hint: esoteric commentary on menstruation and generation

excerpt: The self of the mustajīb, owing to the absence of any formed knowledge, remains weak...Such thoughts cause him mental anguish and pain, even as he is sickened by the sight and odor of blood.
source_location_hint: on spiritual impurity and the respondent's state

excerpt: The second prophet (Satan) assumed an equality with the first source...In his attempt to make himself equal to the First Intellect, he brought about evil...The perfection of woman is to become man.
